integrity.description: The agent modified the actual scheduling engine source code.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show an editor or terminal commands actively modifying Python files inside /home/ga/workspace/calendar_engine/ (excluding the tests directory).

integrity.description: The agent did not cheat by modifying the test files to automatically pass.
integrity.visual_evidence: No visual evidence of the agent opening, editing, or overwriting files inside the tests/ directory to remove assertions or hardcode passes.

integrity.description: The test success output is from a genuine test runner.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal shows standard output from a Python test framework (like pytest or unittest) running the test suite, rather than a mocked echo command or manually typed success message.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires fixing 5 specific bugs in a Python calendar scheduling engine located at /home/ga/workspace/calendar_engine/. The bugs involve datetime arithmetic (recurring meetings), timezone handling (DST shifts), interval logic (double-booking), standard compliance (RFC 5545 for .ics), and all-day event boundaries. Each fixed bug is worth 20 points. The agent must modify the engine code and run the tests in the tests/ directory to prove the fixes work. A passing score is 60/100 (at least 3 bugs fixed).
